confidence.description: Agent's confidence the finding is real in [0, 1]. Persisted in `extra`.

description.description: Canonical evidence-bundle prose. Becomes the signal's `description`.

evidence.description: Citations supporting the finding. Capped at 20 entries.

items.description: One citation attached to a finding. Mirrors `SignalsScoutEvidenceEntry`.

entity_id.description: Optional ID of the cited entity (issue id, recording id, log query id).

summary.description: One-sentence prose about why this evidence supports the finding.

finding_id.description: Stable id for this finding, baked into the signal's source_id for traceability. NOT a dedupe key — re-emitting the same id creates another signal.

hypothesis.description: Optional one-line hypothesis the finding tests.

mcp_trace_id.description: Optional MCP trace id for cross-system debugging.

severity.description:
Optional severity tag — one of P0, P1, P2, P3, P4. Informational only.

* `P0` - P0
* `P1` - P1
* `P2` - P2
* `P3` - P3
* `P4` - P4

tags.description: Optional category tags as lowercase kebab-case slugs (e.g. `cost-spike`, `silent-failure`), max 10. Reuse the vocabulary in your `tags:<domain>:taxonomy` scratchpad entry when a tag fits; coin a new slug when a genuinely new category emerges. Near-miss formats are normalized to slugs; persisted in the signal's `extra.tags` and on the emission row.

date_from.description: ISO-8601 inclusive lower bound for the finding's window.

date_to.description: ISO-8601 inclusive upper bound for the finding's window.

time_range.description: Optional time window the finding refers to.
